verses.fhb_text: In the beginning, our Heavenly Father created the heavens and the earth.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Foundational anchor — Elohim is identified as our Heavenly Father from the canon's first verse.

Renderings:
• "our Heavenly Father" — first occurrence in the book; establishes the Father as foundational per FHB-9.1-1
• "the heavens and the earth" — plural shamayim preserved per FHB-7.9-1

Hebrew/Greek: bereshit bara Elohim (in-beginning created God) → "In the beginning, our Heavenly Father created"
verses.footnote: "Our Heavenly Father" replaces the generic "God" of other English translations to recover the Father-revealing emphasis the Hebrew already carries.

verses.fhb_text: Now the earth was formless and empty, darkness was over the surface of the deep, and the Spirit of our Father was hovering over the waters.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Spirit of Elohim resolves to the Spirit of our Father by pre-Pentecost generic-narration rule.

Renderings:
• "Now" — waw-consecutive opening scene setting per FHB-9.3-1
• "the Spirit of our Father" — pre-Pentecost generic narration, no NT clarification, no Christ-connection in immediate context per FHB-1.2-7(C)
• "hovering" — merachefet preserved; the Spirit's brooding-presence imagery

Hebrew/Greek: ruach Elohim (Spirit of God) → "the Spirit of our Father"

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "Let there be light," and there was light.
verses.decision_notes: Command-and-fulfillment parallelism preserved with comma rather than em dash per FHB-9.8-1.

verses.fhb_text: Our Father saw that the light was good, and he separated the light from the darkness.
verses.decision_notes: Pronoun shift to "he" after "our Father" is established in the verse per FHB-9.2-1.

verses.fhb_text: He called the light "day," and the darkness he called "night." And there was evening, and there was morning — the first day.
verses.decision_notes: Day-closing formula established with em dash before day number per FHB-9.7-1.

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "Let there be a vault between the waters to separate water from water."
verses.decision_notes: New day, new literary unit — re-establish "our Father" per FHB-9.2-1.

verses.fhb_text: So our Father made the vault and separated the water under the vault from the water above it. And it was so.
verses.decision_notes: Consequential waw rendered "So" per FHB-9.3-1; command-fulfillment echo joined with period not em dash per FHB-9.8-1.

verses.fhb_text: He called the vault "sky." And there was evening, and there was morning — the second day.
verses.decision_notes: Day-closing formula preserves the Hebrew parallel structure.

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "Let the water under the sky be gathered to one place, and let dry ground appear." And it was so.
verses.decision_notes: Re-establish "our Father" at the new day per FHB-9.2-1.

verses.fhb_text: He called the dry ground "land," and the gathered waters he called "seas." And our Father saw that it was good.
verses.decision_notes: Plural yammim (seas) preserved per FHB-7.9-1.

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "Let the land produce vegetation: plants bearing seed and trees bearing fruit with seed in it, according to their kinds." And it was so.
verses.decision_notes: Colon introduces the list per FHB-9.5-1 (comma/colon preferred over em dash for simple appositional lists).

verses.fhb_text: The land produced vegetation: plants bearing seed according to their kinds, and trees bearing fruit with seed in it according to their kinds. And our Father saw that it was good.
verses.decision_notes: Result-first construction — "The land produced" leads, as the outcome is the emphasis per FHB-9.4-1.

verses.fhb_text: So there was evening, and there was morning — the third day.
verses.decision_notes: Conjunction varied to "So" per FHB-9.7-1 (do not use the same conjunction for all six days).

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "Let there be lights in the vault of the sky to separate the day from the night, and let them serve as signs to mark sacred times, days, and years.
verses.decision_notes: Re-establish "our Father" at the fourth day per FHB-9.2-1.

verses.fhb_text: And let them be lights in the vault of the sky to give light on the earth." And it was so.
verses.decision_notes: Continuation of the Father's speech from v.14; quotation closes here.

verses.fhb_text: Our Father made two great lights — the greater light to govern the day and the lesser light to govern the night. He also made the stars.
verses.decision_notes: Em dash earns its place as parenthetical clarification ("the greater light... the lesser light") per FHB-9.8-1; "and the stars" rendered as separate sentence rather than dramatic-afterthought em dash per FHB-9.8-1 banned-patterns clause.

verses.fhb_text: He set them in the vault of the sky to give light on the earth,
verses.decision_notes: Pronoun continuation "he" after the Father is the subject of v.16.

verses.fhb_text: to govern the day and the night, and to separate light from darkness. And our Father saw that it was good.
verses.decision_notes: Continuation of v.17 across the verse boundary; modern English keeps the infinitive chain flowing.

verses.fhb_text: And there was evening, and there was morning — the fourth day.
verses.decision_notes: Day-closing formula.

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "Let the water teem with living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the vault of the sky."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: nephesh chayyah for sea-life renders as "living creatures" per FHB-4.5.2-1 (animal referent, not human).

Hebrew/Greek: sheretz nephesh chayyah (swarming living-being) → "living creatures"

verses.fhb_text: So our Father created the great sea creatures and every living creature that moves, with which the water teems, according to their kinds, and every winged bird according to its kind. And our Father saw that it was good.
verses.decision_notes: "Every winged bird" — distributive sense for kol per FHB-7.13-1 (each kind individually); "every living creature" — distributive across kinds. Bara (created) marks this as a fresh act of creation per the Hebrew.

verses.fhb_text: Our Father blessed them and said, "Be fruitful and increase in number and fill the water in the seas, and let the birds increase on the earth."
verses.decision_notes: Father-as-subject opening per FHB-9.4-1 — the blessing is the personal action of the Father.

verses.fhb_text: Then there was evening, and there was morning — the fifth day.
verses.decision_notes: Conjunction varied to "Then" per FHB-9.7-1.

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "Let the land produce living creatures according to their kinds: livestock, creatures that move along the ground, and wild animals, each according to its kind." And it was so.
verses.decision_notes: nephesh chayyah → "living creatures" (animal referent) per FHB-4.5.2-1; colon introduces the list per FHB-9.5-1.

verses.fhb_text: Our Father made the wild animals according to their kinds, the livestock according to their kinds, and all the creatures that move along the ground according to their kinds. And our Father saw that it was good.
verses.decision_notes: "All the creatures" — collective sense for kol per FHB-7.13-1 (totality, not each individually).

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "Let us make humanity in our image, in our likeness, and let them rule over the fish of the sea and the birds of the sky, over the livestock, over all the earth, and over all the creatures that move along the ground."
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: First-person divine plural speech — "let us" / "our image" / "our likeness" preserved verbatim per FHB-6.4.1-1(a).

Renderings:
• "Let us make" — first-person divine plural preserved per FHB-6.4.1-1(a); natural English in spoken voice
• "in our image, in our likeness" — first-person possessive plural preserved per FHB-6.4.1-1(a)
• "humanity" — ha'adam as collective; modern English over the calque "man" or "mankind" per FHB-7.12-1
• "let them rule" — plural verb following collective humanity per FHB-7.9-1

Hebrew/Greek: na'aseh adam b'tzalmenu kid'mutenu (let-us-make humanity in-our-image as-our-likeness) → "Let us make humanity in our image, in our likeness"
verses.footnote: The Father's use of "us" and "our" references Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.

verses.fhb_text: So our Father created humanity in their image; in the image of the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit he created them; male and female he created them.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Trinitarian expansion of Elohim governs the image-of-God noun phrase; the threefold bara parallelism is preserved.

Renderings:
• "our Father created humanity" — narrative verb subject resolves to our Father per FHB-1.2-1 (NOT to the Trinitarian expansion, per FHB-6.4.1-1(b) clarifier — verb subjects follow FHB-1.2-1, not the noun-antecedent expansion)
• "in their image" — b'tzalmo as third-person possessive referencing the divine plural → "their" per FHB-6.4.1-1(c); echoes v.26's "our"
• "in the image of the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit" — b'tzelem Elohim as noun antecedent inside the image-of-God phrase expanded per FHB-6.4.1-1(b)
• "he created them" — collective humanity → "them" consistently per FHB-6.4.1-1(d)
• Semicolons preserve the Hebrew threefold bara parallelism per FHB-9.4-2

Hebrew/Greek: vayivra Elohim et ha'adam b'tzalmo b'tzelem Elohim bara oto zachar u'nekevah bara otam (and-created God humanity in-his-image; in-image-of-God created him; male and-female created them) → FHB rendering above
verses.footnote: Humanity is created in the image of all three Persons — Father, Son, and Holy Spirit — not the Father alone.

verses.fhb_text: Our Father blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and increase in number; fill the earth and subdue it. Rule over the fish of the sea and the birds of the sky and over every living creature that moves on the ground."
verses.decision_notes: Father-as-subject opening per FHB-9.4-1 — the blessing is the Father's personal action toward humanity.

verses.fhb_text: Then our Father said, "I give you every seed-bearing plant on the face of the whole earth and every tree that has fruit with seed in it. They will be yours for food.
verses.decision_notes: "Every" — distributive sense per FHB-7.13-1 (each plant/tree individually); "will be" replaces archaic "shall" per FHB-9.10-1.

verses.fhb_text: And to all the wild animals and all the birds of the sky and all the creatures that move along the ground — everything that has the breath of life in it — I give every green plant for food." And it was so.
verses.decision_notes: Em dash pair earns its place — "everything that has the breath of life in it" is a parenthetical clarifier of the three preceding categories that commas would weaken per FHB-9.8-1; nephesh chayyah in animal context → "the breath of life" per FHB-4.5.2-1.

verses.fhb_text: Our Father saw all that he had made, and it was very good. And there was evening, and there was morning — the sixth day.
verses.decision_notes: "All that he had made" — collective totality per FHB-7.13-1; day-closing formula per FHB-9.7-1.

chapter_notes: Genesis 1 is the canonical Father-anchor for the entire FHB translation per FHB-1.2-1 (Genesis 1 Anchor). "Our Heavenly Father" is established at v.1 per FHB-9.1-1; subsequent references in the chapter use "our Father" with pronoun shifts to "he" within sustained passages per FHB-9.2-1, and "our Father" is re-established at the start of each new creation day per the literary-unit principle. The Spirit of Elohim at v.2 resolves to "the Spirit of our Father" by pre-Pentecost generic-narration clause per FHB-1.2-7(C) — one Spirit, named under his relational angle as the Father's animating presence. The divine plurals at v.26 ("let us... our image... our likeness") are preserved verbatim per FHB-6.4.1-1(a). At v.27, the threefold bara parallelism is preserved with semicolons per FHB-9.4-2; b'tzelem Elohim (the noun-antecedent image-of-God phrase) is expanded to "the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit" per FHB-6.4.1-1(b); b'tzalmo (his image, third-person possessive referencing the divine plural) renders as "their" per FHB-6.4.1-1(c); the collective humanity pronoun renders as "them" consistently per FHB-6.4.1-1(d). nephesh chayyah is rendered "living creature(s)" or "breath of life" in animal contexts per FHB-4.5.2-1 (distinct from "living person" reserved for humans in Gen 2:7). Day-closing formulas at vv.5, 8, 13, 19, 23, 31 use the em dash before the day number per FHB-9.7-1; conjunctions vary across the six days (And/And/So/And/Then/And) per FHB-9.7-1 to avoid mechanical repetition while preserving the Hebrew structural parallel. All three footnote categories that would normally trigger at Genesis 1 (heavenly_father_anchor at v.1, divine_plural_speech at v.26, trinity_image at v.27) are already in footnote_categories_already_used and are therefore not re-footnoted in this chapter.
